provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Bogosi Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Recognition of tribes
provisions.content:
3. Recognition of tribes
(1) The Minister, after consulting a tribal community in its Kgotla , may recognise that tribal community as a tribe.
(2) In deciding whether a tribal community shall be recognised as a tribe, the Minister shall take into account the history, origins, and organisational structure of the community, and any other relevant matters.

provisions.chapter: PART III Recognition and Removal of Dikgosi
provisions.title: Who is a Kgosi

definitions.definition: the institution of traditional leadership or the position of Kgosi
a person recognised as such in terms of section 22(1)
a person so designated by the tribe and recognised as such by the Minister under section 4
the customary meeting place of a tribe or tribal community for the discussion, in terms of customary law, of matters of tribal or communal concern
a person appointed as such in terms of section 10
the person who, according to customary law of that tribe, is responsible for the tribe in the absence of the Kgosi
the area inhabited by a tribe, whether within or outside of a tribal territory
any community or ethnic group, whether or not living within a tribal territory, which is organised in a tribal manner
any territory defined as such in the Tribal Territories Act (Cap. 32:03), and includes the territory defined in Schedule B to the Botswana Boundaries Act (Cap. 03:01)

any tribal community in existence and recognised as a tribe immediately before the commencement of this Act and includes such other tribal communities as may be so recognised under section 3.
(2) A reference in any enactment to "Chief", "Sub-Chief" or any other variant in the nomenclature of "Chief" or "Sub-Chief" shall be construed as a reference to " Kgosi " or any appropriate variation in the nomenclature of " Kgosi ".
